answer: Coptic uses a system of prefixed articles and possessive pronouns that attach to nouns. The genitive relationship is expressed with 'nte'. Numbers follow nouns. There are different forms of possessive pronouns depending on person and number (my, your-singular, your-plural, his, her, our, their). Articles appear to vary by gender and definiteness (p-, t-, ou-). Plural marking can be shown through reduplication or changes in the article.
